title: Use of eval() in Python
description: The use of eval() on user-controlled input can lead to Arbitrary Code Execution (ACE) vulnerabilities.
reasoning: eval() evaluates strings as Python code. If the input comes from a user, they can execute arbitrary system commands, delete files, or exfiltrate data.
suggested_fix: Replace eval() with a safe alternative such as json.loads() if parsing data, or use ast.literal_eval() if specifically needing to parse Python literals safely.
